original_abstract: This study explores the principles and methodologies of Islamic proselytism (da'wah) as articulated in the classical exegesis *Ma'alim al-Tanzil* by Imam al-Baghawy, specifically focusing on Surah An-Nahl, verses 125–128. Employing a qualitative literature-based approach with a *tahlily* (analytical) method, the research examines how al-Baghawy interprets the Quranic mandates for engaging both believers and non-believers. The analysis reveals three core methodological pillars identified in the text: *al-hikmah* (wisdom rooted in the Quran), *al-mau’izhatul hasanah* (good counsel characterized by gentleness), and *jadilhum billati hiya ahsan* (persuasive dialogue conducted without coercion or hostility). Additionally, the study highlights the ethical framework provided in the subsequent verses regarding justice and restraint, emphasizing that patience and forgiveness are superior to proportionate retaliation. The findings conclude that according to al-Baghawy, effective da'wah requires a synergy of strategic communication, emotional intelligence, and personal piety, positioning the preacher not merely as a messenger but as a model of steadfastness and divine consciousness.
humanized_abstract: The paper deals with the principles and methodology of da’wah (Islamization) as elucidated by the classical tafsir, Ma’alim al-Tanzil written by Imam al-Baghawy in which he explained the verse of Surah An-Nash, numbers 125-128. This research has adopted qualitative approach through the method of tahlily (Analytical) in which the researcher made an explanation of the interpretations of the verses mentioned by the author so as to know the methodological approach adopted by the author. It has been proved that the author used three methods in explaining the verse, namely al-hikmah (wisdom that is derived from the Quran), al-mau’izhatul hasanah (good counseling characterized by leniency), and jadilhum billati hiya ahsan (debating without using force or aggression). In addition to that, the author explained the virtues of justice and self-control in the next verses in which he said that patience and forgiveness are better than the proportional punishment for the one who hurts others. Therefore, the researcher reached to the conclusion that da’wah in the opinion of Imam al-Baghawy is based on the unity of three factors: method of communication, moral values, and the individual da’wah preacher’s morality. This leads to that the da’wah preacher turns to be a teacher, a role model, a tolerant and a conscious person of Allah the Almighty.
